subject: Product Management
title: Why PMF Is Not Binary
description: A quick win: understand Why PMF Is Not Binary.

hook.text: Everyone talks about PMF like a switch: you either have it or you don't. Reality is far messier.

buildup.text: Some products have PMF for one segment but not others. Some have it temporarily. Some have it weakly.

discovery.text: PMF is a spectrum. You can have strong fit with early adopters and zero fit with the mainstream market.

twist.text: This is why many startups celebrate too early. Early traction with fans isn't the same as broad market demand.

climax.text: Thinking of PMF as a spectrum helps teams stay humble and keep iterating even when early signs look promising.

punchline.text: PMF isn't a moment. It's a moving target.

quiz.question: Why should PMF be thought of as a spectrum?
options: Products can have fit with some segments but not others, and fit can change over time
PMF is always either fully achieved or not at all
Spectrum thinking helps investors but not product teams
